close_reason: Three types with per-type differences (RSA length check, P-256 format doc) is clearer than a macro. Only 3 types, not a growing set. Premature abstraction per CLAUDE.md standards.
description: Ed25519Signer, EcdsaP256Signer, and RsaPssSigner are structurally identical: same single backend field, identical Debug impl using finish_non_exhaustive(), and identical into_inner(). This is ~80 lines of duplicated boilerplate across three structs. A define_signer! declarative macro would reduce this to ~20 lines and make adding a fourth algorithm a one-liner. Any change to the shared shape (e.g. adding a field, changing Debug format) currently requires three edits.
title: Three identical adapter structs could use a macro
